title: Weak Password Hashing
description:
User passwords are hashed with MD5 before being stored. MD5 is a fast, broken cryptographic hash — rainbow tables and GPU crackers can reverse billions of MD5 hashes per second.

Fix `hash_password()` and `verify_password()` to use bcrypt (a slow, salted adaptive hashing algorithm).
hint: Use `bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode(), bcrypt.gensalt())` for hashing and `bcrypt.checkpw()` for verification.
explanation: MD5 and SHA-1/256 are not suitable for password storage because they are designed to be fast. Passwords need slow, salted algorithms like bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2 that make brute-force attacks computationally expensive.

attack_flow.label: DB breach
attack_flow.value: Attacker extracts MD5 hash from the database

attack_flow.label: Crack attack
attack_flow.value: Rainbow table lookup or GPU cracking (billions of MD5/sec)

why_blocked: bcrypt is intentionally slow (configurable cost factor) and includes a per-password salt. Even identical passwords produce different hashes. A single bcrypt verification takes ~100ms — making brute-force billions of times harder than MD5.
